candidates.survey_name: Public Awareness, Readiness and Capacity Survey (PARCA) - Phase 2
candidates.question_text: To the best of your knowledge, what is the purpose of the Climate Action Incentive Payment (CAIP)? Please make a guess, even if you aren´t sure.

response_options.label: To offset energy related taxes (e.g. carbon tax, etc.)
To help those who are more vulnerable to higher costs (e.g. poor, low income, etc.)
To offset higher costs caused by climate change (e.g. food, gas, etc.)
To offset costs of climate change response
To help with cost of home upgrades (e.g. insulation, solar panels, appliances, etc.)
To increase awareness of climate change / Educate people (e.g. causes, impact, solutions, etc.)
To encourage / Incentivize people to take action (e.g. fight climate change, live greener, etc.)
It is politically motivated / Not to help the environment (e.g. scam, tax grab, for votes, etc.)

candidates.survey_name: Public Awareness, Readiness and Capacity Survey (PARCA) - Phase 2
candidates.question_text: Below are some potential negative effects of planting more trees in cities, towns, and other communities. Please rate how import - Other (please specify)

response_options.label: Worsening allergy symptoms (e.g. by creating more pollen)
More tree litter (e.g., fallen leaves, seeds, fruit)
Increasing risk of property or infrastructure damage from falling trees and/or branches
Increasing risk of property or infrastructure damage from root growth (e.g., sidewalks, water and sewer pipes)
Increasing the number of trees that could burn during a wildfire
Worsening safety concerns (e.g., feeling less safe walking at night)
Increasing risk that low-income residents may be forced to move as property values increase
More conflict between people and wildlife
Trees are not important / Create problems

candidates.survey_name: Public Awareness, Readiness and Capacity Survey (PARCA) - Phase 2
candidates.question_text: Why do you choose to buy bottled water instead of drinking tap water from a reusable bottle? Please select all that apply.

response_options.label: Water fountains or taps aren’t always available
It has fewer chemicals, like fluoride
I sometimes forget my reusable bottle
Don't want to drink my city / Town water
When I'm out / Away from home
I use it in the car
For my appliances / Machines (e.g. coffee maker, sleep machine, humidifier, etc.)
For emergencies / Outages / As a backup
I use large refillable containers / Use a water cooler
It's cost effective (e.g. cheaper, we buy in bulk, on sale, etc.)
I don't buy bottled water / Clicked on the wrong response
